epigraph.text: Cauliflower is nothing but cabbage with a college education.

follyOfTheDay.headline: HOA Fines Homeowner $500 for Shadow That Wasn't Pre-Approved
follyOfTheDay.summary: A homeowners association in California has assessed a $500 fine against a resident for the shadow cast by his mailbox—a shadow which, according to board minutes, had not been submitted for aesthetic approval. The decision reflects a broader philosophy among HOA leadership that all shadows, being visible alterations to the community's visual landscape, require advance architectural review and directional certification. The board noted in its ruling that while the homeowner had permission to install the mailbox itself, he had failed to account for the photometric consequences of that installation. When the resident inquired whether he should also seek approval for his own shadow, he was told to "not be difficult."
follyOfTheDay.commentary: That the HOA was entirely serious, and that the resident paid, suggests both parties have given up on the concept of proportionality.
